title: New Client Template
description: Schema-first onboarding. Today this is a checklist; later it can become a form that creates a real project record with placeholder integrations.
noteBody: Use this as the standard intake. Each field maps directly to a ProjectRecord property — adding values later is a JSON edit until we wire a creation form.

intro.title: Why this exists
intro.body: Every new client needs the same structured baseline so the dashboard, operations view, and renewals tracking work from day one. Filling these in manually now keeps the data clean while we build out automation.

groups.title: Identity
groups.description: Who the client is and how the project is labelled across the portfolio.

fields.label: Project name
fields.hint: Display name shown across the dashboard.

fields.label: Project ID (slug)
fields.hint: Used in URLs and as the primary key. Lowercase, hyphenated.

fields.label: Tier
fields.hint: Starter / Business / Premium / Agency.

fields.label: Tags
fields.hint: Free-form pills used for filtering, e.g. live, demo, hospitality.

fields.label: Lead owner
fields.hint: Primary point of contact internally.

fields.label: Support owner
fields.hint: Backup / paired owner.

groups.title: Hosting & Infrastructure
groups.description: The technical surface area we operate.

fields.label: Live URL
fields.hint: Production website URL.

fields.label: Staging URL
fields.hint: Preview / staging deploy URL.

fields.label: Hosting provider
fields.hint: Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, etc.

fields.label: Repo URL
fields.hint: GitHub repo for the site source.

fields.label: DNS provider
fields.hint: Where the zone records live.

fields.label: Registrar
fields.hint: Where the domain is registered.

groups.title: Renewals
groups.description: Tracked centrally on the Operations view.

fields.label: Domain expiry
fields.hint: Free-form, e.g. 'Mar 2027'. Renewal alerts derive from this.

fields.label: SSL expiry
fields.hint: Free-form. Surfaced on the Operations renewals panel.

fields.label: Contacts
fields.hint: Add one per stakeholder: name, role, email, phone, notes.

fields.label: Credentials
fields.hint: Reference vault locations only — never store actual secrets here.

groups.title: Operational Content
groups.description: Optional but powerful. These power the client workspace.

fields.label: Quick links
fields.hint: Hosting, repo, DNS, vault, analytics. Drives the Quick Actions bar.

fields.label: Health checks
fields.hint: What to monitor (homepage, SSL, contact form, DNS).

fields.label: Priorities
fields.hint: Current focus areas / open work.

fields.label: Tasks
fields.hint: Action queue items with owner, priority, due.

nextSteps.title: 1. Create the JSON record
nextSteps.description: Append a new project to data/admin/projects.json using the schema above. Required fields: id, name, lead, support, liveUrl, githubOrg.

nextSteps.title: 2. Add to navigation
nextSteps.description: The dashboard, operations view, and renewals panel will pick the project up automatically once it's in the file.

nextSteps.title: 3. Fill the workspace gradually
nextSteps.description: A 30%-filled record is still useful. Missing fields are surfaced as 'setup needed' so you always know what to add next.

nextSteps.title: 4. Future automation
nextSteps.description: When integrations land, deployments, SSL, and uptime will populate themselves — but the manual record stays the source of truth.
